problems.question:
ALGORITHM Mystery(n)
//Input: A nonnegative integer n
S ← 0
for i ← 1 to n do
S ← S + i * i
return S

explanation.text: Identify the basic operation of the algorithm (e.g. comparisons, assignments).
Set up a summation representing the execution count of the basic operation based on input size n.
Solve the summation using standard arithmetic or geometric series formulas.
Determine the asymptotic growth class (O, Θ, Ω) using limits or definitions.

problems.question:
ALGORITHM Secret(A[0..n-1])
//Input: An array A[0..n-1] of n real numbers
minval ← A[0]; maxval ← A[0]
for i ← 1 to n - 1 do
  if A[i] < minval
    minval ← A[i]
  if A[i] > maxval
    maxval ← A[i]
return maxval - minval

explanation.text: Identify the basic operation of the algorithm (e.g. comparisons, assignments).
Set up a summation representing the execution count of the basic operation based on input size n.
Solve the summation using standard arithmetic or geometric series formulas.
Determine the asymptotic growth class (O, Θ, Ω) using limits or definitions.

problems.question:
ALGORITHM Enigma(A[0..n − 1, 0..n − 1])
//Input: A matrix A[0..n − 1, 0..n − 1] of real numbers
for i ← 0 to n − 2 do
for j ← i + 1 to n − 1 do
if A[i, j] ≠ A[j, i]
return false
return true

explanation.text: Identify the basic operation of the algorithm (e.g. comparisons, assignments).
Set up a summation representing the execution count of the basic operation based on input size n.
Solve the summation using standard arithmetic or geometric series formulas.
Determine the asymptotic growth class (O, Θ, Ω) using limits or definitions.
